sourceMovie.title: The Darjeeling Limited
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Darjeeling Limited works as a adventure movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The adventure and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Captain Fantastic
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Darjeeling Limited clicked for you, Captain Fantastic (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Death at a Funeral
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Darjeeling Limited clicked for you, Death at a Funeral (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Darjeeling Limited?
faq.answer: Try Captain Fantastic, Death at a Funeral, Benny & Joon—each shares DNA with The Darjeeling Limited while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Darjeeling Limited?
faq.answer: The Darjeeling Limited (2007) is best described as Adventure, Drama, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Darjeeling Limited part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Darjeeling Limited Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
